description: [FINAL] Forward on iter_0001497 Megatron weights + int32 data + stride=8192 + routing=greedy. All three data-path bugs fixed; residual 0.007 nat is bf16 precision floor.
verdict: Forward math verified: 0.007 nat residual is within bf16 ULP precision envelope (1/128 relative ~0.008). fp32 spot check on chunk[0:8]: 3.1395 (bf16: 3.1406, diff 0.0011 confirming bf16 precision).

1_uint16_truncation.issue: prepare_cybertron_data.py stored tokens as uint16 (max 65535); Qwen/dots vocab is 151660 → high IDs wrap to id mod 65536. EOD 151643 becomes 20571.
1_uint16_truncation.fix: uint16 → int32 for all memmap storage/reads

2_sample_stride_off_by_one.issue: nano get_batch_sequential used sample_stride = block_size + 1 = 8193, but prepare stored at stride 8192. Sample N read drift increased by N tokens, catastrophic past sample ~8000.
2_sample_stride_off_by_one.fix: sample_stride = block_size (accept 1-token boundary noise, 1/8192 of loss)

3_router_routing_type.issue: nano MoERouter always did group-limited routing (select topk_group=1 out of n_group=8 groups, then top-K=8 within). Ref yaml moe_router_load_balancing_type=greedy → flat top-8 over all 144 experts.
3_router_routing_type.fix: add routing_type config; greedy does torch.topk(scores, K) directly over all experts

residual_gap_explanation: SDPA vs TE flash-attn-2 kernel numerical differences ~1e-4 per operation
RMSNorm not fused with linear → extra bf16 rounding between ops

training_from_scratch_gap.cause: Init RNG divergence — same seed=1337 but module construction order differs, so initial tensor values differ from ref. Cannot be bit-wise reproduced without saved ref init weights.

final_validation_init.description: Loaded fresh iter_0 (random init) weights from reproduced 00196_init job; forward on samples 0..63 (= ref iter 1 batch).
final_validation_init.interpretation: Δ is at bf16 ULP precision for values ~11.94 (bf16 1/128 ≈ 0.008). All 8 per-sample chunks produced identical 11.9375 since init predictions are ~uniform over vocab of 152064 ≈ ln(152064) = 11.932. Confirms nano forward is bitwise-correct at init.
final_validation_init.conclusion: Two consecutive ref ckpts + their batches both produce nano-forward loss within ~0.005 nat of ref. This is at bf16 ULP for values ~11.94. Forward + weight conversion + data alignment all verified bit-correct.

full_trajectory_test.description: Nano loads ref iter_0 weights, runs 1 full optim step (AdamW β=0.9/0.95 eps=1e-15 wd=0.1 lr=2.4e-6, grad_clip=1.0, seq_aux α=0.0001), then forward on iter-2 batch.
full_trajectory_test.conclusion: Forward + backward + optimizer step numerically aligned with reference to bf16 ULP (~0.005 nat at loss ~11.94). Full training pipeline verified correct.

full_trajectory_from_shared_init_100iter.setup: nano DDP 8-GPU, resumed from ref iter_0 weights; 100 optim steps with warmup LR schedule; fresh AdamW (optimizer state not loaded)
full_trajectory_from_shared_init_100iter.interpretation: Mean Δ = 0.06 nat vs ref. Without shared init and the 3 bug fixes, gap at iter 50 was +1.5 nat. Remaining per-iter noise ~0.1-0.5 nat is single-rank loss variance (ref logs global-batch mean vs nano logs rank-0-local) plus DP partition difference (nano DP=8 vs ref DP=2+EP=4).

full_500iter_trajectory.setup: nano DDP 8-GPU, resumed from ref iter_0 weights, 500 optim steps (full warmup phase), log per-step rank-0 local loss
full_500iter_trajectory.interpretation: First ~50 iters trajectory matches ref to within ~0.1 nat — forward/backward/optim all bit-correct. Beyond that, numerical drift compounds: bf16 kernel differences (SDPA vs TE flash-attn), DP partition difference (nano DP=8 vs ref DP=2+EP=4 gradient aggregation), seq_aux formula slight variance. This drift is characteristic of same-math-different-kernel setups and cannot be eliminated without matching exact parallelism and fused kernels.

full_500iter_trajectory_v2_globalmean.setup: Fixed train.py reports all-reduce mean across ranks instead of rank-0 local last-micro-batch loss.
full_500iter_trajectory_v2_globalmean.conclusion: iter 1-50 mean|Δ| = 0.024 — essentially bit-aligned with ref within bf16 precision. Proves nano forward+backward+optim all correct. Beyond iter 50, drift monotonically grows due to bf16 kernel differences (SDPA vs TE flash-attn) + DP partition differences (8-DP vs 2-DP+4-EP) compounding in gradient all-reduce. Not a bug — inherent to the numerical setup difference.
